name: Sysmon service error report
definition: Sysmon Event ID 255 records an internal Sysmon error condition.
trigger_scenarios: Microsoft documents that this event is generated when an error occurs within Sysmon, including heavy load, tasks that could not be performed, a Sysmon service bug, or security and integrity conditions not being met.

key_fields.explanation: The error identifier and description explain which Sysmon component failed. Microsoft Q&A examples include RuleEngine and ServiceThread descriptions for schema incompatibility.
Use the timestamp to measure telemetry gaps around the error.
Prioritize hosts where Sysmon errors affect critical telemetry coverage.

false_positives: High system load and Sysmon bugs can generate this event without attacker activity.
Configuration schema mismatches after upgrades can produce RuleEngine or ServiceThread errors until the configuration is rebuilt.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

detection_notes: Use threshold=1 on high-value hosts when Event 255 contains ID=DriverCommunication, RuleEngine, ServiceThread, or another error that creates a telemetry gap. Microsoft documents Event 255 for Sysmon internal errors and notes security/integrity conditions may be involved; treat it as T1562.001 only when it follows service stop, configuration change, driver unload, or unexplained loss of Events 1/3/11. Correlate with Events 4 and 16 and verify whether the Sysmon schema/configuration version changed before escalating.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 255
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
sample_log:
UtcTime: 2026-07-13 03:08:00.000
ID: RuleEngine
Description: Registry rule version 4.22 is incompatible with Sysmon rule version 4.30
Computer: HOST01

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 255
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 255
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 255
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=255
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the documented field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 255
assumptions: Confirm Provider is Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 255
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 255
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 255
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the event-specific conditions and correlations documented on this page.

value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The actual error description is required to distinguish load, bug, and integrity conditions.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret Description with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 255 mean?
faqs.answer: Sysmon encountered an internal error caused by load, a software defect, or a security or integrity condition.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 255 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Operational overload and product defects can generate it.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 255 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Review the exact error description, Sysmon version, host load, configuration, and nearby Events 4 and 16.

sources.title: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools (T1562.001)
